title: ANH12068 CT Scan
text_en: In some cases, you may receive contrast dye before your procedure in a drink, an injection, or a barium anema to make it easier for your doctor to see certain areas of your body.

title: ANH12068 CT Scan
text_en: If you received a drink with contrast dye, your esophagus or stomach will be highlighted.

title: ANH12068 CT Scan
text_en: If you received an injection, your blood vessels, gallbladder, liver, or urinary tract will be highlighted.

title: ANH12068 CT Scan
text_en: If you received a barium anema, your large intestine will be highlighted.

title: ANH12068 CT Scan
text_en: After the procedure, you can resume your normal activities. If you received contrast dye, drink plenty of fluids to help your kidneys remove the dye from your body.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: A pressure injury or ulcer is a wound caused by constant pressure on your skin. It may also be called a pressure sore, pressure ulcer, bed sore, or de-cubitus ulcer.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: Pressure injuries occur most frequently in areas where your bones are close to the skin, including your heels, ankles, hips, tailbone, or elbows.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: In order to understand how pressure injuries happen, it's important to know about the layers of the skin. Your skin is made up of three main layers.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: The top layer called the epidermis is a thin layer that protects your body from the environment.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: The thicker middle layer is called the dermis. Its main functions are to supply the epidermis with nutrients, help regulate your body temperature, and provide strength and elasticity to your skin.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: The innermost layer is called the hypodermis, or subcutaneous layer. This layer stores fat, which cushions the muscles and bones beneath it.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: In a pressure injury, constant pressure on the skin over a long period of time can compress the blood vessels that supply the layers with the oxygen and nutrients they need to survive. If there's not enough blood flow, your skin tissue begins to die and a pressure injury

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: If you have a pressure injury, your healthcare provider will classify it based on what can be known about the depth of tissue damage. This is called staging.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: In a stage 1 pressure injury, the skin is intact, red, and inflamed. The redness is non-blanchable. This means it doesn't become pale if you press on it.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: A stage 2 pressure injury appears as a blister or an open sore. There is partial thickness loss of the epidermis and dermis. The areas surrounding the sore may be red and irritated.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: A stage 3 pressure injury is a crater-like, full thickness loss of skin. It extends down to the fat tissue of the hypodermis.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: Stage 4 is the most severe. A stage 4 pressure injury is a full thickness loss of tissue. It extends through all three layers of skin and into the underlying muscle or bone.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: An unstageable pressure injury is a full thickness loss of tissue. It's called unstageable because the injury is covered by dead tissue, making it hard to tell how deep it is.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: And a suspected deep tissue injury appears as a maroon or purple area of the skin. It may contain a blister filled with blood. It looks like this because of damaged soft tissue underneath your skin.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: To prevent infection in your pressure injury, your doctor will clean your wound using water and a mild soap or saline solution.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: If your wound is severe, your doctor will debris or surgically remove the dead tissue from the pressure injury.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: To protect your wound and keep it from drying out while it heals, your doctor may cover it with a specialized bandage.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
text_en: To find out more about pressure injuries, talk to your healthcare provider.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Every time you smoke a cigarette, toxic gases pass into your lungs, then into your blood stream, where they spread to every organ in your body.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: A cigarette is made using the tobacco leaf, which contains nicotine and a variety of other compounds. As the tobacco and compounds burn, they release thousands of dangerous chemicals, including over 40 known to cause cancer. Cigarette smoke contains the poisonous gases carbon monoxide and nitrogen oxide, as well as trace amounts of cancer-causing radioactive particles.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: All forms of tobacco are dangerous, including cigars, pipes, and smokeless tobacco, such as chewing tobacco and snuff.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Nicotine is an addictive chemical in tobacco. After you inhale tobacco smoke, nicotine flows through the bloodstream to your brain, where it induces a pleasurable feeling. When you repeatedly expose your brain to nicotine, it becomes desensitized, making you crave more and more nicotine just to feel normal.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Smoking causes death. People who smoke typically die at an earlier age than non-smokers. In fact, one of every five deaths in the United States is linked to cigarette smoking. If you smoke your risk of major health problems increases dramatically, including heart disease, heart attack, stroke, lung cancer, and death from chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Smoking causes cardiovascular disease. When nicotine flows through your adrenal glands, it stimulates the release of epinephrine, a hormone that raises your blood pressure. In addition, nicotine and carbon monoxide can damage the lining of the inner walls in your arteries.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
text_en: Smoking also raises your risk for blood clots, because it causes platelets in your blood to clump together.
